data.title: Neil Mehta Lifts Talos Energy Price Target to $17 on Accretive Gulf of Mexico Acquisition and Strengthened Offshore Portfolio
data.description:
Talos Energy, the Energy sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Neil Mehta from Goldman Sachs maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $17.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Neil Mehta has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Talos Energy’s strategic positioning and growth prospects. He underscores that the planned joint acquisition of deepwater Gulf of Mexico assets from Shell, alongside a Ridgewood Energy affiliate, meaningfully enhances Talos’s offshore portfolio at an attractive $850 million net cash consideration.

He notes that management expects the transaction to be immediately accretive across key financial measures, reinforcing the company’s earnings and cash flow profile once closed by year‑end 2026, subject to customary conditions. In light of this improved outlook and a constructive M&A backdrop for E&P companies, Mehta raises his price target from $15 to $17 while reiterating a Buy rating to reflect the stronger asset base and anticipated value creation for shareholders.

In another report released today, KeyBanc also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $21.00 price target.

TALO’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $11.200 to $12.910, which is a 15.27% increase.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Sagimet Therapeutics: Buy Rating Affirmed as Clinically De‑Risked FASN Franchise Advances; Price Target Raised to $31
data.description:
Sagimet Biosciences, Inc. Class A, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Debanjana Chatterjee from JonesTrading maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $31.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Debanjana Chatterjee has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, notably Sagimet’s lead asset denifanstat, a first-in-class FASN inhibitor, which has already shown success in a Phase 3 acne trial in China and is moving toward a pivotal U.S. Phase 3 program in the second half of 2026. This late-stage, clinically de-risked asset underpins a sizable commercial opportunity, supporting the upward revision of the price target to $31 as the program advances toward a late‑2027 readout.

Chatterjee’s positive view also reflects the potential upside from Sagimet’s follow‑on compound TVB‑3567, which is expected to deliver key sebum‑reduction data from an ongoing Phase 1 trial and could demonstrate superior efficacy to denifanstat in proof‑of‑concept acne studies. In addition, plans for a topical FASN inhibitor, with an IND anticipated around early 2028, could broaden the franchise across multiple acne severities and extend Sagimet’s reach in dermatology, reinforcing the Buy recommendation.

Chatterjee covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Spyre Therapeutics, AbCellera Biologics, and EyePoint Pharmaceuticals. According to TipRanks, Chatterjee has an average return of 56.2% and a 75.88% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released on June 22, Wedbush also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $28.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Harrow Health, Citing Expanding Ophthalmic Franchise and Long-Term Growth Pipeline
data.description:
William Blair analyst Lachlan Hanbury Brown has maintained their bullish stance on HROW stock, giving a Buy rating today.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Lachlan Hanbury Brown has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Harrow Health’s expanding ophthalmic franchise and recent execution milestones. The commercial introduction of Byooviz, through an exclusive U.S. partnership with Samsung Bioepis, broadens Harrow’s presence in the large anti-VEGF retina market and adds a new, albeit initially modest, revenue contributor.

Beyond Byooviz, Brown highlights Harrow’s growing portfolio of FDA-approved products, including Vevye, Iheezo, and Triesence, which together reinforce the company’s positioning in U.S. ophthalmic pharmaceuticals. While acknowledging that periodic demand patterns may cause share-price fluctuations, he views the company’s execution and product pipeline—such as future commercialization of Opuviz—as underpinning a compelling long-term growth profile that supports a Buy recommendation.

Hanbury Brown covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Harrow Health, Viridian Therapeutics, and Ocular Therapeutix. According to TipRanks, Hanbury Brown has an average return of 1.3% and a 41.30% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released today, Lake Street also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $60.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
